lesson.title: Common semantic errors in C++

exercises.title: Diagnose and Fix a Running Total with Semantic Bugs
exercises.prompt_md:
## Goal
Complete the function `int computeSum(int nums[], int count)` so it correctly sums all elements of the array and returns the result without any semantic errors.

## Input
- Line 1: integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 100), the number of integers.
- Lines 2..N+1: one integer per line, each in the range [-1000, 1000].

## Output
Print exactly two lines:
- Line 1: `Sum: X` where X is the sum of all N integers.
- Line 2: `Average: Y` where Y is the average formatted to exactly one decimal place (e.g. `Average: 3.5`).

No extra blank lines.

## Requirements
1. `computeSum` must use an index-based loop with a signed `int` loop variable and the `count` parameter as the bound.
2. The loop bound must be strictly less than the count (no off-by-one: do not use `<=`).
3. The accumulator variable must be initialized to `0` at the point of declaration.
4. The average must be computed by casting `computeSum`'s return value to `double` before dividing — do not perform integer division.
5. Dividing by N must use `static_cast<double>` on at least one operand.
6. Use `std::cout` with `std::fixed` and `std::setprecision(1)` to format the average to one decimal place.
7. Hidden tests cover N = 1 (single element), all negative values, and a mix of positive and negative values that sum to zero.

## Notes
- Allowed headers: `<iostream>`, `<iomanip>`.

test_cases.label: hidden — values that sum to zero

exercises.title: Flag Out-of-Range Scores Using Bitwise Check Without Precedence Bugs
exercises.prompt_md:
## Goal
Complete the function `void reportScores(int scores[], int count)` that processes a list of exam scores, prints each score with a status tag, and then prints summary statistics — all without semantic errors.

## Input
- Line 1: integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 50), the number of scores.
- Lines 2..N+1: one integer per line representing a score, each in the range [0, 200].

## Output
For each score (in order), print one line:
`Score X: PASS` if the score is in [60, 100] (inclusive on both ends), otherwise `Score X: FAIL`.

After all per-score lines, print two more lines:
- `Total: T` where T is the sum of all scores.
- `Pass count: P` where P is the number of PASS scores.

No extra blank lines.

## Requirements
1. Use a signed `int` loop variable with `count` as the bound — no signed/unsigned comparison warnings.
2. The PASS/FAIL decision must use this exact bitwise expression to determine whether a score is out of range, with explicit parentheses to prevent precedence bugs: `bool outOfRange = ((score < 60) | (score > 100)) != 0;` — use `outOfRange` (or its negation) to choose the tag. Note: in production code you would use `||` instead of `|`, but this exercise deliberately uses bitwise OR to practise recognizing the precedence issue.
3. The sum accumulator must be initialized to `0` at its declaration (uninitialized variable error prevention).
4. The pass counter must be initialized to `0` at its declaration.
5. `Total` must be computed as the sum of all scores regardless of PASS/FAIL status.
6. Hidden tests cover N = 1 with a boundary score of exactly 60, N = 1 with a score of exactly 100, and a list where every score fails.

## Notes
- Allowed headers: `<iostream>`.

test_cases.label: sample — mixed scores
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Score 90: PASS
Score 55: FAIL
Score 100: PASS
Score 30: FAIL
Score 75: PASS
Total: 350
Pass count: 3

test_cases.label: hidden — all scores fail
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Score 150: FAIL
Score 0: FAIL
Score 101: FAIL
Total: 251
Pass count: 0

fixes_applied.description: Replaced std::vector<int> with C-style arrays (int nums[] / int scores[]) in both exercises to remove forward reference to Ch 16/17
Removed const reference parameters (const std::vector<int>&) which are Ch 12 material; replaced with plain array parameters (int nums[], int count)
Replaced std::println / <print> (C++23) with std::cout in both exercises
Exercise 9.3.1: replaced <print> and <vector> includes with <iomanip>; use std::fixed/std::setprecision(1) for formatted output
Exercise 9.3.2: reduced includes to just <iostream>; all output uses std::cout with string concatenation
Rewrote summary code example to use C-style array and std::cout instead of std::vector and std::println; removed mentions of std::ssize() and C++20 ranges
Added explicit note in exercise 9.3.2 prompt that bitwise OR is used deliberately for the exercise and || is preferred in production
